body:
1. Right now the orchestrator is automatically attached after each action, and is gated with a flag. 
    1. this caused quite some UX issues: when users want to just launch /review but not run followup work, this is less useful. 
    2. also people do not know what’s going on 
2. A proper way: 
    1. orchestrator should be as a separate high-level route: 
        1. user can use tag /orchestrate (or some other name) to start this mode 
        2. or label /orchestrate to start 
    2. this means the orchestrator should be aware of the current situation 
        1. so we should have some situation aware planning when we just start the orchestrator: when we just launch the orchestrator, it should check the status and launch the right subagent.   


@sepo-agent /answer plan?

comments.body:
No broader tracker/parent was referenced from issue #19, and I didn’t find related open issues beyond #19.

Current architecture (relevant files):
- Explicit mention/label routes are resolved in [`triage.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/triage.ts) and applied in [`resolve-dispatch.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/resolve-dispatch.ts).
- Trigger extraction is in [`extract-context.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/extract-context.ts).
- Routing and route jobs live in [`agent-router.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml), with label entry via [`agent-label.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-label.yml).
- Orchestrator is currently post-action only, dispatched from:
  - [`agent-implement.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-implement.yml)
  - [`agent-review.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-review.yml)
  - [`agent-fix-pr.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-fix-pr.yml)
  through [`dispatch-agent-orchestrator.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/dispatch-agent-orchestrator.ts), then executed by [`agent-orchestrator.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-orchestrator.yml) and [`orchestrate-handoff.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts).

Proposed implementation approach:
1. Make orchestrator a first-class route (`/orchestrate` + `agent/orchestrate`) in triage/router, same pattern as `/review` and `agent/review`.
2. Remove automatic post-action orchestrator dispatch from implement/review/fix-pr workflows.
3. Add a “manual-start orchestration” path in `agent-orchestrator.yml` with situation-aware bootstrap:
   - PR target: choose review vs fix-pr based on current PR state (and planner in `agent` mode).
   - Issue target: default to implement unless an active linked PR is detected.
4. Keep existing handoff/dedupe policy code in [`handoff.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/handoff.ts) for chained automation, but make it opt-in via explicit orchestrate route.
5. Update tests/docs to reflect the new UX contract.

Execution plan (for approval):
1. Extend routing grammar and label mapping for `orchestrate` in [`triage.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/triage.ts), with tests in [`triage.test.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/__tests__/triage.test.ts).
2. Update context extraction + label trigger handling for `agent/orchestrate` in [`extract-context.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/extract-context.ts) and [`agent-label.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-label.yml).
3. Add an `orchestrate` route job to [`agent-router.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml) that dispatches [`agent-orchestrator.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-orchestrator.yml) explicitly.
4. Introduce manual-start inputs/logic in [`agent-orchestrator.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-orchestrator.yml) and a small CLI helper (new file) for situation detection.
5. Remove automatic “Orchestrate automation handoff” steps from [`agent-implement.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-implement.yml), [`agent-review.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-review.yml), and [`agent-fix-pr.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-fix-pr.yml).
6. Update docs in [`agent-orchestrator.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md), [`supported-workflows.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md), and [`agent-actions.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/actions/agent-actions.md).
7. Update envelope coverage tests in [`envelope.test.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/__tests__/envelope.test.ts) and run the test suite.

Please confirm and I’ll start coding this plan.  
One clarification before implementation: do you want to support both command aliases (`/orchestrate` and `/orchestrator`) or only `/orchestrate`?

**Sepo implementation finished**

- Branch: `agent/implement-issue-19/codex-25067966881`
- Pull request: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/20

Implemented issue #19 by redesigning orchestration as an explicit top-level route: added deterministic `/orchestrate` and `agent/orchestrate` routing/label support, wired a dedicated orchestrate job in `agent-router.yml`, updated orchestrator dispatch/runtime to handle manual starts with situation-aware target checks (issue -> implement, PR + CHANGES_REQUESTED -> fix-pr, otherwise review), and removed automatic post-action orchestrator handoff from implement/review/fix-pr workflows. Updated tests and docs to reflect the new UX and routing model. Verification run: `npm --prefix .agent run build` and `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/triage.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js` (both passing).

title: Redesign the orchestrator system
